notes: No TTS or audio generation runs inside the child app.
English narration is the default demo experience; Korean narration remains optional.
Apps resolve outputFile from this manifest; story JSON can stay stable as audio improves.
Synthetic drafts must be replaced or reviewed before production release.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 깊은 산골 마을에 오누이가 어머니와 함께 따끈한 떡을 쌌어요. 하얀 김이 방 안을 감싸고, 떡마다 집 냄새가 포근히 배었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In a deep mountain village, two siblings helped Mother wrap warm rice cakes before market day. Steam curled around the room, and every cake smelled like home.

candidates.text: In a deep mountain village, two siblings helped Mother wrap warm rice cakes before market day. Steam curled around the room, and every cake smelled like home.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 깊은 산골 마을에 오누이가 어머니와 함께 따끈한 떡을 쌌어요. 하얀 김이 방 안을 감싸고, 떡마다 집 냄새가 포근히 배었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In a deep mountain village, two siblings helped Mother wrap warm rice cakes before market day. Steam curled around the room, and every cake smelled like home.

candidates.text: 깊은 산골 마을에 오누이가 어머니와 함께 따끈한 떡을 쌌어요. 하얀 김이 방 안을 감싸고, 떡마다 집 냄새가 포근히 배었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니가 광주리를 등에 메며 말했어요. “집도 잘 지키고, 서로도 잘 지키렴.” 누나는 두 번 고개를 끄덕였고, 동생은 한 번 더 크게 끄덕였어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother lifted the basket onto her back. “Watch the house, and watch each other,” she said. The older sister nodded twice, and the younger brother nodded once more.

candidates.text: Mother lifted the basket onto her back. “Watch the house, and watch each other,” she said. The older sister nodded twice, and the younger brother nodded once more.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니가 광주리를 등에 메며 말했어요. “집도 잘 지키고, 서로도 잘 지키렴.” 누나는 두 번 고개를 끄덕였고, 동생은 한 번 더 크게 끄덕였어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother lifted the basket onto her back. “Watch the house, and watch each other,” she said. The older sister nodded twice, and the younger brother nodded once more.

candidates.text: 어머니가 광주리를 등에 메며 말했어요. “집도 잘 지키고, 서로도 잘 지키렴.” 누나는 두 번 고개를 끄덕였고, 동생은 한 번 더 크게 끄덕였어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 높은 소나무 그늘에서 호랑이가 길을 지켜보고 있었어요. 그는 웃지 않았어요. 배고픈 코가 찬 공기 속에서 벌름거리고, 눈은 광주리를 따라 움직였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: High in the pines, a tiger watched the road. He did not smile. His eyes followed the basket, and his hungry nose twitched in the cold air.

candidates.text: High in the pines, a tiger watched the road. He did not smile. His eyes followed the basket, and his hungry nose twitched in the cold air.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 높은 소나무 그늘에서 호랑이가 길을 지켜보고 있었어요. 그는 웃지 않았어요. 배고픈 코가 찬 공기 속에서 벌름거리고, 눈은 광주리를 따라 움직였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: High in the pines, a tiger watched the road. He did not smile. His eyes followed the basket, and his hungry nose twitched in the cold air.

candidates.text: 높은 소나무 그늘에서 호랑이가 길을 지켜보고 있었어요. 그는 웃지 않았어요. 배고픈 코가 찬 공기 속에서 벌름거리고, 눈은 광주리를 따라 움직였지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 눈 내리듯 소리 없이 길로 내려왔어요. “어머니, 안녕하시오.” 그는 믿기엔 너무 매끄러운 목소리로 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger stepped onto the path as softly as falling snow. “Good evening, Mother,” he said, in a voice too smooth to trust.

candidates.text: The tiger stepped onto the path as softly as falling snow. “Good evening, Mother,” he said, in a voice too smooth to trust.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 눈 내리듯 소리 없이 길로 내려왔어요. “어머니, 안녕하시오.” 그는 믿기엔 너무 매끄러운 목소리로 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger stepped onto the path as softly as falling snow. “Good evening, Mother,” he said, in a voice too smooth to trust.

candidates.text: 호랑이는 눈 내리듯 소리 없이 길로 내려왔어요. “어머니, 안녕하시오.” 그는 믿기엔 너무 매끄러운 목소리로 말했지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니는 집에서 기다리는 아이들을 떠올렸어요. 떡 하나를 언덕 아래로 던지자, 호랑이가 그쪽으로 몸을 날렸고 어머니는 반대쪽으로 달렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother remembered her children waiting at home. She tossed one rice cake down the hill. The tiger lunged after it, and Mother ran the other way.

candidates.text: Mother remembered her children waiting at home. She tossed one rice cake down the hill. The tiger lunged after it, and Mother ran the other way.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니는 집에서 기다리는 아이들을 떠올렸어요. 떡 하나를 언덕 아래로 던지자, 호랑이가 그쪽으로 몸을 날렸고 어머니는 반대쪽으로 달렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother remembered her children waiting at home. She tossed one rice cake down the hill. The tiger lunged after it, and Mother ran the other way.

candidates.text: 어머니는 집에서 기다리는 아이들을 떠올렸어요. 떡 하나를 언덕 아래로 던지자, 호랑이가 그쪽으로 몸을 날렸고 어머니는 반대쪽으로 달렸어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 떡을 덥석 먹고, 어머니가 떨어뜨린 수건 냄새를 맡았어요. 그러고는 초가집 불빛을 바라보며 속삭였지요. “익숙한 목소리라면 작은 문도 열리겠군.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger snapped up the cake, then sniffed Mother’s fallen scarf. He looked toward the cottage lights. “Ah,” he whispered. “Little doors open for familiar voices.”

candidates.text: The tiger snapped up the cake, then sniffed Mother’s fallen scarf. He looked toward the cottage lights. “Ah,” he whispered. “Little doors open for familiar voices.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 떡을 덥석 먹고, 어머니가 떨어뜨린 수건 냄새를 맡았어요. 그러고는 초가집 불빛을 바라보며 속삭였지요. “익숙한 목소리라면 작은 문도 열리겠군.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger snapped up the cake, then sniffed Mother’s fallen scarf. He looked toward the cottage lights. “Ah,” he whispered. “Little doors open for familiar voices.”

candidates.text: 호랑이는 떡을 덥석 먹고, 어머니가 떨어뜨린 수건 냄새를 맡았어요. 그러고는 초가집 불빛을 바라보며 속삭였지요. “익숙한 목소리라면 작은 문도 열리겠군.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 집에서는 오누이가 창호지에 비친 별을 세고 있었어요. 그때 문이 톡, 톡, 톡 울렸어요. “얘들아, 문 열어라.” 밖에서 목소리가 들렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At home, the children counted stars on the paper window. Then came a knock: tap, tap, tap. “Open the door, my children,” called a voice from outside.

candidates.text: At home, the children counted stars on the paper window. Then came a knock: tap, tap, tap. “Open the door, my children,” called a voice from outside.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 집에서는 오누이가 창호지에 비친 별을 세고 있었어요. 그때 문이 톡, 톡, 톡 울렸어요. “얘들아, 문 열어라.” 밖에서 목소리가 들렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At home, the children counted stars on the paper window. Then came a knock: tap, tap, tap. “Open the door, my children,” called a voice from outside.

candidates.text: 집에서는 오누이가 창호지에 비친 별을 세고 있었어요. 그때 문이 톡, 톡, 톡 울렸어요. “얘들아, 문 열어라.” 밖에서 목소리가 들렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 동생이 달려가려 하자 누나가 소매를 살짝 붙잡았어요. “기다려.” 누나가 속삭였어요. “엄마 목소리는 따뜻한데, 저 목소리는 솔잎처럼 까슬까슬해.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The younger brother ran forward, but his sister caught his sleeve. “Wait,” she whispered. “Mother’s voice is warm. That voice scratches like pine bark.”

candidates.text: The younger brother ran forward, but his sister caught his sleeve. “Wait,” she whispered. “Mother’s voice is warm. That voice scratches like pine bark.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 동생이 달려가려 하자 누나가 소매를 살짝 붙잡았어요. “기다려.” 누나가 속삭였어요. “엄마 목소리는 따뜻한데, 저 목소리는 솔잎처럼 까슬까슬해.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The younger brother ran forward, but his sister caught his sleeve. “Wait,” she whispered. “Mother’s voice is warm. That voice scratches like pine bark.”

candidates.text: 동생이 달려가려 하자 누나가 소매를 살짝 붙잡았어요. “기다려.” 누나가 속삭였어요. “엄마 목소리는 따뜻한데, 저 목소리는 솔잎처럼 까슬까슬해.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “손을 보여 주세요.” 누나가 말했어요. 달빛 속으로 발 하나가 쓱 들어왔어요. 넓고, 줄무늬가 있고, 결코 엄마 손이 아니었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Show us your hand,” said the sister. A paw slid into the moonlight — wide, striped, and not Mother’s hand at all.

candidates.text: “Show us your hand,” said the sister. A paw slid into the moonlight — wide, striped, and not Mother’s hand at all.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “손을 보여 주세요.” 누나가 말했어요. 달빛 속으로 발 하나가 쓱 들어왔어요. 넓고, 줄무늬가 있고, 결코 엄마 손이 아니었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Show us your hand,” said the sister. A paw slid into the moonlight — wide, striped, and not Mother’s hand at all.

candidates.text: “손을 보여 주세요.” 누나가 말했어요. 달빛 속으로 발 하나가 쓱 들어왔어요. 넓고, 줄무늬가 있고, 결코 엄마 손이 아니었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 발을 얼른 감췄어요. “장에 다녀와 손이 얼었단다.” 그가 달콤하게 꾸몄지요. 누나는 바닥을 가리켰어요. 문 아래로 줄무늬 꼬리가 말려 들어오고 있었거든요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger pulled the paw back. “My hand is cold from market,” he crooned. The sister pointed to the floor. A striped tail was curling under the door.

candidates.text: The tiger pulled the paw back. “My hand is cold from market,” he crooned. The sister pointed to the floor. A striped tail was curling under the door.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 발을 얼른 감췄어요. “장에 다녀와 손이 얼었단다.” 그가 달콤하게 꾸몄지요. 누나는 바닥을 가리켰어요. 문 아래로 줄무늬 꼬리가 말려 들어오고 있었거든요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 문소리가 더 커졌어요. 톡. 톡. 쿵. 밖의 그림자가 고개를 들자, 아이들은 엄마에게 있을 리 없는 귀를 보았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The knock grew louder. Tap. Tap. TAP. The shadow outside lifted its head, and the children saw ears that did not belong to Mother.

candidates.text: The knock grew louder. Tap. Tap. TAP. The shadow outside lifted its head, and the children saw ears that did not belong to Mother.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 문소리가 더 커졌어요. 톡. 톡. 쿵. 밖의 그림자가 고개를 들자, 아이들은 엄마에게 있을 리 없는 귀를 보았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The knock grew louder. Tap. Tap. TAP. The shadow outside lifted its head, and the children saw ears that did not belong to Mother.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 누나는 동생의 손을 꼭 잡았어요. “뒤 창문으로 가자.” 누나가 숨소리만큼 작게 말했어요. “발은 조용히, 마음은 씩씩하게.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The older sister took her brother’s hand. “To the back window,” she breathed. “Quiet feet. Brave hearts.”

candidates.text: The older sister took her brother’s hand. “To the back window,” she breathed. “Quiet feet. Brave hearts.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 누나는 동생의 손을 꼭 잡았어요. “뒤 창문으로 가자.” 누나가 숨소리만큼 작게 말했어요. “발은 조용히, 마음은 씩씩하게.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밖에는 감나무가 어둡고 높게 서 있었어요. 누나가 먼저 올라가 손을 내밀었어요. “내가 딛는 곳만 밟아.” 누나가 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Outside stood the persimmon tree, dark and tall. The sister climbed first, then reached down. “Step where I step,” she said.

candidates.text: Outside stood the persimmon tree, dark and tall. The sister climbed first, then reached down. “Step where I step,” she said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 마당으로 뛰어나와 위를 올려다보았어요. 다정한 목소리는 사라졌지요. “내려오너라.” 그가 으르렁댔어요. “떡도 함께 가져오고.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger burst into the yard and looked up. His gentle voice was gone. “Come down,” he growled, “and bring the rice cakes too.”

candidates.text: The tiger burst into the yard and looked up. His gentle voice was gone. “Come down,” he growled, “and bring the rice cakes too.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 마당으로 뛰어나와 위를 올려다보았어요. 다정한 목소리는 사라졌지요. “내려오너라.” 그가 으르렁댔어요. “떡도 함께 가져오고.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 동생의 손이 떨렸어요. 누나는 그 손을 세 번 꼭 쥐었어요. 한 번은 용기, 한 번은 조용히, 한 번은 나랑 함께 있어.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The younger brother trembled. His sister squeezed his hand three times. Once for courage, once for quiet, once for stay with me.

candidates.text: The younger brother trembled. His sister squeezed his hand three times. Once for courage, once for quiet, once for stay with me.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 동생의 손이 떨렸어요. 누나는 그 손을 세 번 꼭 쥐었어요. 한 번은 용기, 한 번은 조용히, 한 번은 나랑 함께 있어.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 나무를 오르기 시작했어요. 나무껍질이 긁히고 잎이 흔들렸지요. 가지 사이로 배고프고 영악한 눈이 번쩍였지만, 아이들은 두려움보다 더 높은 곳을 바라보았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger began to climb. Bark scratched. Leaves shook. His eyes shone between the branches, hungry and clever, but the children looked higher than fear.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger began to climb. Bark scratched. Leaves shook. His eyes shone between the branches, hungry and clever, but the children looked higher than fear.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “하늘님, 우리 말을 들어 주세요.” 누나가 속삭였어요. “우리 약속이 진짜였다면, 달빛 같은 동아줄을 내려 주세요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Sky above, hear us,” whispered the sister. “Send a rope as silver as moonlight, if our promise was true.”

candidates.text: “Sky above, hear us,” whispered the sister. “Send a rope as silver as moonlight, if our promise was true.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 달빛처럼 부드럽고 약속처럼 튼튼한 은빛 동아줄이 내려왔어요. 오누이는 손에 손을 이어 밝은 밤하늘로 올라갔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Down came a silver rope, soft as light and strong as a promise. The children climbed, hand over hand, into the moon-bright sky.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 위를 보고 미안한 척 말했어요. “하늘님, 저에게도 줄을 내려 주시오.” 하지만 달도 그의 목소리를 알아들은 듯했어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger looked up and tried to sound sorry. “Dear sky,” he called, “send me a rope too.” But even the moon seemed to know his voice.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 새끼줄 하나가 호랑이 발 앞으로 내려왔어요. 어둠 속에서는 튼튼해 보였지만, 짚은 하늘이 아니라 들판을 기억하는 법이지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A straw rope dropped into his paws. It looked strong in the dark, but straw remembers the field, not the sky.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger climbed and climbed. Halfway up, the straw whispered, ssuk, ssuk, ssuk. Then it frayed like dry grass.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 둥, 하고 북보다 부드럽게 호랑이는 두꺼운 솔잎 더미 위에 떨어졌어요. 재채기를 한 번, 두 번 하더니, 그 많던 꾀 많은 말도 모두 잊어버렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: With a thump softer than a drum, the tiger landed in a deep pile of pine needles. He sneezed once, twice, and forgot all his clever words.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니가 마을 사람들의 등불과 함께 달려왔어요. 떨어진 수건, 발자국, 그리고 아이들이 남긴 작은 용기의 길을 따라온 거예요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother came running with village lanterns bobbing behind her. She had followed the broken scarf, the paw prints, and the brave little trail her children left.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The villagers rang brass bells and clapped wooden blocks. The tiger shook pine needles from his whiskers and ran so far that his stripes looked like shadows.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 높은 하늘에서 오누이는 은빛 동아줄을 붙잡고 아래를 보았어요. 마을은 작아 보였지만, 어머니의 등불은 사랑만큼 크게 보였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: High above, the children held the silver rope and looked down. The village looked tiny, but Mother’s lantern looked as big as love.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “모든 아이가 집으로 가는 빛을 찾게 해 주세요.” 누나가 말했어요. “모든 어두운 길이 아침을 기억하게 해 주세요.” 동생이 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “May every child find a light home,” said the sister. “May every dark road remember morning,” said the brother.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 하늘은 두 가지 빛으로 대답했어요. 밤을 지키는 은빛, 아침을 여는 금빛. 오누이의 용기는 그 두 빛 속에서 반짝였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The sky answered in two colors: silver for the watchful night, gold for the brave morning. The children’s courage shone in both.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 닭이 울자 은빛 동아줄은 아이들을 어머니 품으로 내려 주었어요. 해가 떠오르고 달은 안전한 비밀처럼 조용히 옅어졌지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: When the rooster called, the silver rope lowered the children into Mother’s arms. The sun rose, and the moon faded softly, like a secret kept safe.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: After that night, the children still helped with rice cakes. But whenever dusk came, they checked the door together: voice, hand, tail, shadow.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The village told the story every market day. “Quiet feet,” they would say. “Brave hearts. And never open the door for a voice that scratches.”

candidates.text: Long ago, in a blue-green valley, a woodcutter polished an old iron axe every morning. It had belonged to his father, and it fit his hands like a memory.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 옛날 푸른 골짜기에 나무꾼이 살았어요. 그는 아침마다 낡은 쇠도끼를 닦았지요. 그 도끼는 아버지가 쓰던 것이어서 손에 추억처럼 꼭 맞았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Long ago, in a blue-green valley, a woodcutter polished an old iron axe every morning. It had belonged to his father, and it fit his hands like a memory.

candidates.text: 옛날 푸른 골짜기에 나무꾼이 살았어요. 그는 아침마다 낡은 쇠도끼를 닦았지요. 그 도끼는 아버지가 쓰던 것이어서 손에 추억처럼 꼭 맞았어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood. “Take only what you need,” he told the forest.

candidates.text: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood. “Take only what you need,” he told the forest.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood. “Take only what you need,” he told the forest.

candidates.text: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 어느 날, 도끼가 손에서 미끄러졌어요. 풍덩! 도끼는 일거리와 추억과 저녁 밥걱정까지 데리고 연못 속으로 가라앉았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: One misty day, his axe slipped — plunk! Down it sank into the pond, taking his work, his memory, and his supper with it.

candidates.text: One misty day, his axe slipped — plunk! Down it sank into the pond, taking his work, his memory, and his supper with it.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 어느 날, 도끼가 손에서 미끄러졌어요. 풍덩! 도끼는 일거리와 추억과 저녁 밥걱정까지 데리고 연못 속으로 가라앉았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: One misty day, his axe slipped — plunk! Down it sank into the pond, taking his work, his memory, and his supper with it.

candidates.text: 안개 낀 어느 날, 도끼가 손에서 미끄러졌어요. 풍덩! 도끼는 일거리와 추억과 저녁 밥걱정까지 데리고 연못 속으로 가라앉았지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 연못가에 무릎을 꿇었어요. “오랜 친구야.” 그가 속삭였어요. “어떻게 널 집에 데려가지?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The woodcutter knelt by the water. “Old friend,” he whispered, “how will I bring you home?”

candidates.text: The woodcutter knelt by the water. “Old friend,” he whispered, “how will I bring you home?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 연못가에 무릎을 꿇었어요. “오랜 친구야.” 그가 속삭였어요. “어떻게 널 집에 데려가지?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The woodcutter knelt by the water. “Old friend,” he whispered, “how will I bring you home?”

candidates.text: 나무꾼은 연못가에 무릎을 꿇었어요. “오랜 친구야.” 그가 속삭였어요. “어떻게 널 집에 데려가지?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 연못이 은은히 빛나기 시작했어요. 안개 같은 소매와 오래된 돌처럼 다정한 눈을 가진 산신령이 물 위로 올라왔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pond began to shine. Up rose a mountain spirit with sleeves like mist and eyes as kind as old stones.

candidates.text: The pond began to shine. Up rose a mountain spirit with sleeves like mist and eyes as kind as old stones.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 연못이 은은히 빛나기 시작했어요. 안개 같은 소매와 오래된 돌처럼 다정한 눈을 가진 산신령이 물 위로 올라왔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pond began to shine. Up rose a mountain spirit with sleeves like mist and eyes as kind as old stones.

candidates.text: 연못이 은은히 빛나기 시작했어요. 안개 같은 소매와 오래된 돌처럼 다정한 눈을 가진 산신령이 물 위로 올라왔지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요. 너무 반짝여서 나무꾼은 아주 잠깐 숨 쉬는 것도 잊었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Did you drop this?” asked the spirit, lifting an axe of gold. It shone so brightly the woodcutter forgot to breathe for one small blink.

candidates.text: “Did you drop this?” asked the spirit, lifting an axe of gold. It shone so brightly the woodcutter forgot to breathe for one small blink.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요. 너무 반짝여서 나무꾼은 아주 잠깐 숨 쉬는 것도 잊었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Did you drop this?” asked the spirit, lifting an axe of gold. It shone so brightly the woodcutter forgot to breathe for one small blink.

candidates.text: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요. 너무 반짝여서 나무꾼은 아주 잠깐 숨 쉬는 것도 잊었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그러다 그는 자기 손을 내려다보았어요. 그 손은 낡은 쇠도끼의 무게를 기억하고 있었지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Then he looked at his hands. They remembered the weight of the old iron axe. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

candidates.text: Then he looked at his hands. They remembered the weight of the old iron axe. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그러다 그는 자기 손을 내려다보았어요. 그 손은 낡은 쇠도끼의 무게를 기억하고 있었지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Then he looked at his hands. They remembered the weight of the old iron axe. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

candidates.text: 그러다 그는 자기 손을 내려다보았어요. 그 손은 낡은 쇠도끼의 무게를 기억하고 있었지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령은 미소 지으며 은도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 겨울 눈 위의 달빛처럼 번쩍였지요. “그럼 이것이 네 것이냐?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The spirit smiled and lifted a silver axe. It flashed like moonlight on winter snow. “Then is this one yours?”

candidates.text: The spirit smiled and lifted a silver axe. It flashed like moonlight on winter snow. “Then is this one yours?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령은 미소 지으며 은도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 겨울 눈 위의 달빛처럼 번쩍였지요. “그럼 이것이 네 것이냐?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The spirit smiled and lifted a silver axe. It flashed like moonlight on winter snow. “Then is this one yours?”

candidates.text: 산신령은 미소 지으며 은도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 겨울 눈 위의 달빛처럼 번쩍였지요. “그럼 이것이 네 것이냐?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 은도끼는 참 아름다웠어요. 하지만 아름다운 것과 내 것인 것은 달랐지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The silver axe was beautiful. But beautiful was not the same as belonging. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

candidates.text: The silver axe was beautiful. But beautiful was not the same as belonging. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 은도끼는 참 아름다웠어요. 하지만 아름다운 것과 내 것인 것은 달랐지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The silver axe was beautiful. But beautiful was not the same as belonging. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

candidates.text: 은도끼는 참 아름다웠어요. 하지만 아름다운 것과 내 것인 것은 달랐지요. “아닙니다. 제 도끼는 낡은 쇠도끼입니다.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 산신령이 평범한 쇠도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 날은 조금 닳고 오랜 일로 어둑했지요. 나무꾼의 얼굴이 해 뜨듯 밝아졌어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At last the spirit lifted a plain iron axe, nicked at the edge and dark from years of work. The woodcutter’s face lit like sunrise.

candidates.text: At last the spirit lifted a plain iron axe, nicked at the edge and dark from years of work. The woodcutter’s face lit like sunrise.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 산신령이 평범한 쇠도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 날은 조금 닳고 오랜 일로 어둑했지요. 나무꾼의 얼굴이 해 뜨듯 밝아졌어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At last the spirit lifted a plain iron axe, nicked at the edge and dark from years of work. The woodcutter’s face lit like sunrise.

candidates.text: 마침내 산신령이 평범한 쇠도끼를 들어 올렸어요. 날은 조금 닳고 오랜 일로 어둑했지요. 나무꾼의 얼굴이 해 뜨듯 밝아졌어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “그것이 제 도끼입니다.” 그가 말했어요. “반짝여서가 아니라, 제 곁에서 함께 일해 왔으니까요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “That is mine,” he said. “Not because it shines, but because it has worked beside me.”

candidates.text: “That is mine,” he said. “Not because it shines, but because it has worked beside me.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “그것이 제 도끼입니다.” 그가 말했어요. “반짝여서가 아니라, 제 곁에서 함께 일해 왔으니까요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “That is mine,” he said. “Not because it shines, but because it has worked beside me.”

candidates.text: “그것이 제 도끼입니다.” 그가 말했어요. “반짝여서가 아니라, 제 곁에서 함께 일해 왔으니까요.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령이 부드럽게 웃었어요. “정직한 손은 보물보다 큰 것을 들 수 있구나.” 그는 세 도끼를 모두 나무꾼에게 주었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The spirit laughed softly. “A truthful hand can carry more than treasure.” He gave the woodcutter all three axes.

candidates.text: The spirit laughed softly. “A truthful hand can carry more than treasure.” He gave the woodcutter all three axes.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령이 부드럽게 웃었어요. “정직한 손은 보물보다 큰 것을 들 수 있구나.” 그는 세 도끼를 모두 나무꾼에게 주었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The spirit laughed softly. “A truthful hand can carry more than treasure.” He gave the woodcutter all three axes.

candidates.text: 산신령이 부드럽게 웃었어요. “정직한 손은 보물보다 큰 것을 들 수 있구나.” 그는 세 도끼를 모두 나무꾼에게 주었어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 깊이 절했어요. 그는 금도끼를 팔아 마을 다리를 고치고, 쇠도끼는 평범한 아침들을 위해 간직했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The woodcutter bowed low. He sold the gold axe, repaired the village bridge, and kept the iron axe for ordinary mornings.

candidates.text: The woodcutter bowed low. He sold the gold axe, repaired the village bridge, and kept the iron axe for ordinary mornings.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 나무꾼은 깊이 절했어요. 그는 금도끼를 팔아 마을 다리를 고치고, 쇠도끼는 평범한 아침들을 위해 간직했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The woodcutter bowed low. He sold the gold axe, repaired the village bridge, and kept the iron axe for ordinary mornings.

candidates.text: 나무꾼은 깊이 절했어요. 그는 금도끼를 팔아 마을 다리를 고치고, 쇠도끼는 평범한 아침들을 위해 간직했지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 한 이웃이 그 이야기를 듣고 가슴속에 따끔한 욕심이 자라는 걸 느꼈어요. “금을 주는 연못이라고?” 그가 말했지요. “참 흥미롭군.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A neighbor heard the story and felt a prickly little want grow under his ribs. “A pond that gives gold?” he said. “How interesting.”

candidates.text: A neighbor heard the story and felt a prickly little want grow under his ribs. “A pond that gives gold?” he said. “How interesting.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 한 이웃이 그 이야기를 듣고 가슴속에 따끔한 욕심이 자라는 걸 느꼈어요. “금을 주는 연못이라고?” 그가 말했지요. “참 흥미롭군.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A neighbor heard the story and felt a prickly little want grow under his ribs. “A pond that gives gold?” he said. “How interesting.”

candidates.text: 한 이웃이 그 이야기를 듣고 가슴속에 따끔한 욕심이 자라는 걸 느꼈어요. “금을 주는 연못이라고?” 그가 말했지요. “참 흥미롭군.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그는 번쩍이는 새 도끼를 빌려 연못으로 성큼성큼 갔어요. 그리고 가장 큰 소리가 나도록 풍덩 빠뜨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: He borrowed a shiny new axe, marched to the pond, and dropped it in with the loudest splash he could make.

candidates.text: He borrowed a shiny new axe, marched to the pond, and dropped it in with the loudest splash he could make.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그는 번쩍이는 새 도끼를 빌려 연못으로 성큼성큼 갔어요. 그리고 가장 큰 소리가 나도록 풍덩 빠뜨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: He borrowed a shiny new axe, marched to the pond, and dropped it in with the loudest splash he could make.

candidates.text: 그는 번쩍이는 새 도끼를 빌려 연못으로 성큼성큼 갔어요. 그리고 가장 큰 소리가 나도록 풍덩 빠뜨렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령이 다시 올라왔어요. 이번에는 미소가 조금 덜했지요. “이 금도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The mountain spirit rose again, not smiling quite as much. “Did you drop this golden axe?”

candidates.text: The mountain spirit rose again, not smiling quite as much. “Did you drop this golden axe?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 산신령이 다시 올라왔어요. 이번에는 미소가 조금 덜했지요. “이 금도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The mountain spirit rose again, not smiling quite as much. “Did you drop this golden axe?”

candidates.text: 산신령이 다시 올라왔어요. 이번에는 미소가 조금 덜했지요. “이 금도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “네!” 이웃은 너무 빨리 외쳤어요. 그 말은 마음이 붙잡기도 전에 튀어나와 버렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Yes!” cried the neighbor too quickly. The word jumped out before his heart could catch it.

candidates.text: “Yes!” cried the neighbor too quickly. The word jumped out before his heart could catch it.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “네!” 이웃은 너무 빨리 외쳤어요. 그 말은 마음이 붙잡기도 전에 튀어나와 버렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Yes!” cried the neighbor too quickly. The word jumped out before his heart could catch it.

candidates.text: “네!” 이웃은 너무 빨리 외쳤어요. 그 말은 마음이 붙잡기도 전에 튀어나와 버렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 연못은 조용해졌어요. 금도끼도 가라앉고, 은도끼도 가라앉고, 빌린 도끼도 바닥에 그대로 남았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pond went quiet. The gold axe sank away, the silver axe sank away, and the borrowed axe stayed at the bottom too.

candidates.text: The pond went quiet. The gold axe sank away, the silver axe sank away, and the borrowed axe stayed at the bottom too.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pond went quiet. The gold axe sank away, the silver axe sank away, and the borrowed axe stayed at the bottom too.

candidates.text: 연못은 조용해졌어요. 금도끼도 가라앉고, 은도끼도 가라앉고, 빌린 도끼도 바닥에 그대로 남았지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 이웃은 털썩 주저앉았어요. “아.” 그가 속삭였지요. “풍덩 소리만 따라 했지, 정직은 따라 하지 않았구나.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The neighbor sat down hard. “Oh,” he whispered. “I copied the splash, but not the truth.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 이웃은 털썩 주저앉았어요. “아.” 그가 속삭였지요. “풍덩 소리만 따라 했지, 정직은 따라 하지 않았구나.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The neighbor sat down hard. “Oh,” he whispered. “I copied the splash, but not the truth.”

candidates.text: 이웃은 털썩 주저앉았어요. “아.” 그가 속삭였지요. “풍덩 소리만 따라 했지, 정직은 따라 하지 않았구나.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 다음 날 아침, 이웃은 갈퀴를 들고 돌아와 연못 길의 낙엽을 치웠어요. 그는 금을 달라고 하지 않았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The next morning, he came back with a rake and cleaned leaves from the pond path. He did not ask for gold.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: After many honest mornings, the spirit returned the borrowed axe. “Truth grows slowly,” the spirit said, “but it grows strong.”

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The woodcutter and the neighbor fixed roofs, stacked winter wood, and laughed about the day the pond taught louder than any teacher.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Every morning, the old iron axe still shone a little — not with gold, not with silver, but with being cared for.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the edge of a sleepy village, a tiger practiced his scariest face in a puddle. The puddle was not impressed.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “오늘 밤엔 누군가 내 울음소리에 벌벌 떨겠지.” 호랑이가 말했어요. 그가 으르렁대자 귀뚜라미가 찍, 대답했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Tonight,” said the tiger, “someone will tremble at my roar.” He roared. A cricket answered, chirp.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 초가집에서 아기 울음소리가 들렸어요. “아하!” 호랑이가 말했지요. “드디어 누군가 내가 가까이 온 걸 아는구나.”

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger did not see snacks. He saw a whole army. “Forgive me, mighty persimmons,” he breathed.

candidates.text: 호랑이는 사과 연습을 했어요. “곶감님, 죄송합니다.” 너무 컸어요. “곶감님, 죄송합니다.” 너무 삐죽했어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 아침이 되자 마을 사람들은 빙글빙글 돈 발자국과, 소매에서 지푸라기를 털어 내는 어리둥절한 나그네를 보았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: By morning, the village found paw prints running in circles and one very confused traveler brushing straw from his sleeves.

candidates.text: By morning, the village found paw prints running in circles and one very confused traveler brushing straw from his sleeves.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 어머니는 새 곶감을 처마 밑에 매달았어요. “말리는 거란다.” 어머니가 아기에게 말했어요. 아기는 달콤한 주황 미소를 지었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother hung new persimmons under the eaves. “They are for drying,” she told the baby. The baby smiled a sticky orange smile.

candidates.text: Mother hung new persimmons under the eaves. “They are for drying,” she told the baby. The baby smiled a sticky orange smile.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Mother hung new persimmons under the eaves. “They are for drying,” she told the baby. The baby smiled a sticky orange smile.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 숲 가장자리에서 호랑이는 처마 쪽으로 절했어요. 한 번도, 두 번도 아니고, 조심조심 일곱 번이나요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From the forest edge, the tiger bowed to the eaves. Not once. Not twice. Seven careful times.

candidates.text: From the forest edge, the tiger bowed to the eaves. Not once. Not twice. Seven careful times.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From the forest edge, the tiger bowed to the eaves. Not once. Not twice. Seven careful times.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 한 아이가 물었어요. “호랑이가 왜 절해요?” 할머니가 말했지요. “어떤 가르침은 달고, 어떤 가르침은 쫄깃하단다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A child asked, “Why is the tiger bowing?” Grandmother said, “Some lessons are sweet. Some are chewy.”

candidates.text: A child asked, “Why is the tiger bowing?” Grandmother said, “Some lessons are sweet. Some are chewy.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 다시는 그 집을 귀찮게 하지 않았어요. 다만 주황 등불, 둥근 달, 수상한 호박 하나는 멀리했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger never bothered that house again. He did, however, avoid orange lanterns, round moons, and one suspicious pumpkin.

candidates.text: The tiger never bothered that house again. He did, however, avoid orange lanterns, round moons, and one suspicious pumpkin.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 바람이 곶감 줄을 흔들면 톡, 톡, 톡 소리가 났어요. 멀리서 그 소리를 들은 호랑이는 또 그 말을 속삭였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: When wind shook the dried persimmons, they bumped softly: tok, tok, tok. The tiger heard it from far away and whispered his line.

candidates.text: When wind shook the dried persimmons, they bumped softly: tok, tok, tok. The tiger heard it from far away and whispered his line.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: When wind shook the dried persimmons, they bumped softly: tok, tok, tok. The tiger heard it from far away and whispered his line.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “곶감님, 죄송합니다.” 호랑이가 말했어요. 이번에도 곶감들은 달콤하고 조용하게 흔들릴 뿐이었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “Forgive me, mighty persimmon,” he said. This time, the persimmons only swayed, sweet and silent.

candidates.text: “Forgive me, mighty persimmon,” he said. This time, the persimmons only swayed, sweet and silent.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “곶감님, 죄송합니다.” 호랑이가 말했어요. 이번에도 곶감들은 달콤하고 조용하게 흔들릴 뿐이었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 아기는 졸려졌어요. 어머니가 곶감 한 조각을 주자, 초가집은 눈처럼 조용해졌지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The baby grew sleepy. Mother gave one tiny bite of gotgam, and the whole cottage became quiet as snow.

candidates.text: The baby grew sleepy. Mother gave one tiny bite of gotgam, and the whole cottage became quiet as snow.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The baby grew sleepy. Mother gave one tiny bite of gotgam, and the whole cottage became quiet as snow.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그리고 산 어딘가에서 호랑이는 꼬리를 발 위에 감고 잠들었어요. 너무 힘세게 들리는 간식이 조금 적은 세상을 꿈꾸면서요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And somewhere in the hills, a tiger tucked his tail around his paws and dreamed of a world with fewer snacks that sounded so powerful.

candidates.text: And somewhere in the hills, a tiger tucked his tail around his paws and dreamed of a world with fewer snacks that sounded so powerful.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And somewhere in the hills, a tiger tucked his tail around his paws and dreamed of a world with fewer snacks that sounded so powerful.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 한 마을에 두 형제가 살았어요. 흥부는 그릇은 작아도 문은 열려 있었고, 놀부는 곳간은 가득해도 덧문은 꼭 닫혀 있었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Two brothers lived in one village: Heungbu, whose bowl was small but whose door was open, and Nolbu, whose barns were full but whose shutters stayed shut.

candidates.text: Two brothers lived in one village: Heungbu, whose bowl was small but whose door was open, and Nolbu, whose barns were full but whose shutters stayed shut.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Two brothers lived in one village: Heungbu, whose bowl was small but whose door was open, and Nolbu, whose barns were full but whose shutters stayed shut.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 배고픈 이웃이 찾아와 “부스러기라도 조금 있을까요?” 하고 속삭였어요. 흥부는 주먹밥 반쪽을 나누었지요. 많은 건 아니었어요. 하지만 그에게 있는 전부였답니다.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: When a hungry neighbor came by and whispered, “Any crumb to spare?” Heungbu shared half a rice ball. It was not much. It was also all he had.

candidates.text: When a hungry neighbor came by and whispered, “Any crumb to spare?” Heungbu shared half a rice ball. It was not much. It was also all he had.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 골목 건너편에서 놀부는 항아리를 세고 있었어요. “문을 열면 바람도 한 숟가락 달라 하겠지.” 그가 중얼거렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Across the lane, Nolbu counted his jars. “If I open the door,” he muttered, “the wind might ask for a spoon.”

candidates.text: Across the lane, Nolbu counted his jars. “If I open the door,” he muttered, “the wind might ask for a spoon.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 골목 건너편에서 놀부는 항아리를 세고 있었어요. “문을 열면 바람도 한 숟가락 달라 하겠지.” 그가 중얼거렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Across the lane, Nolbu counted his jars. “If I open the door,” he muttered, “the wind might ask for a spoon.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 봄이 제비와 함께 돌아왔어요. 제비는 흥부네 지붕을 돌며 하늘을 목 안에 담은 듯 노래했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Spring returned with a swallow. It circled Heungbu’s roof and sang as if the whole sky fit in its throat.

candidates.text: Spring returned with a swallow. It circled Heungbu’s roof and sang as if the whole sky fit in its throat.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 바람 센 어느 오후, 제비가 떨어졌어요. 아이들이 숨을 삼켰고, 흥부는 떨어진 노래를 받치듯 작은 새를 두 손에 담았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: One windy afternoon, the swallow fell. Heungbu’s children gasped, and Heungbu cupped the tiny bird like a dropped song.

candidates.text: One windy afternoon, the swallow fell. Heungbu’s children gasped, and Heungbu cupped the tiny bird like a dropped song.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 흥부는 마지막 깨끗한 천으로 제비 다리를 감싸 주었어요. “날개가 있어도 손님은 손님이지.” 그가 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Heungbu used his last clean cloth for the bird’s leg. “A guest is a guest,” he said, “even with wings.”

candidates.text: Heungbu used his last clean cloth for the bird’s leg. “A guest is a guest,” he said, “even with wings.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 날마다 제비는 힘을 되찾았어요. 다시 날아오른 아침, 제비는 흥부 손바닥에 씨앗 하나를 떨어뜨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Day by day, the swallow grew stronger. On the morning it flew away, it dropped one seed into Heungbu’s palm.

candidates.text: Day by day, the swallow grew stronger. On the morning it flew away, it dropped one seed into Heungbu’s palm.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 날마다 제비는 힘을 되찾았어요. 다시 날아오른 아침, 제비는 흥부 손바닥에 씨앗 하나를 떨어뜨렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 씨앗은 평범하고 작았어요. “자랄 수 있으면 자라 보렴.” 흥부는 그렇게 속삭이며 씨앗을 심었지요. 작은 것들은 늘 그의 마음으로 가는 길을 알고 있었거든요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The seed was plain and small. “Grow if you can,” Heungbu whispered, planting it anyway, because small things had always known the way to his heart.

candidates.text: The seed was plain and small. “Grow if you can,” Heungbu whispered, planting it anyway, because small things had always known the way to his heart.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 아침엔 덩굴이 지붕을 올랐고, 낮엔 잎이 마당에 그늘을 만들었어요. 저녁엔 둥근 박들이 달처럼 흔들렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: By morning, a vine had climbed the roof. By noon, leaves shaded the yard. By evening, round gourds rocked like moons.

candidates.text: By morning, a vine had climbed the roof. By noon, leaves shaded the yard. By evening, round gourds rocked like moons.

candidates.text: 아침엔 덩굴이 지붕을 올랐고, 낮엔 잎이 마당에 그늘을 만들었어요. 저녁엔 둥근 박들이 달처럼 흔들렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 흥부가 톱을 들었어요. 아이들이 손뼉으로 장단을 맞췄지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Heungbu lifted his saw. The children clapped the rhythm: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

candidates.text: Heungbu lifted his saw. The children clapped the rhythm: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 흥부가 톱을 들었어요. 아이들이 손뼉으로 장단을 맞췄지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Heungbu lifted his saw. The children clapped the rhythm: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

candidates.text: 흥부가 톱을 들었어요. 아이들이 손뼉으로 장단을 맞췄지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 첫 번째 박이 후우 하고 열렸어요. 쌀과 이불과 버선과 햇빛 냄새 나는 따뜻한 옷들이 쏟아졌지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The first gourd opened with a sigh. Out poured rice, blankets, socks, and warm clothes that smelled of sunlight.

candidates.text: The first gourd opened with a sigh. Out poured rice, blankets, socks, and warm clothes that smelled of sunlight.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 첫 번째 박이 후우 하고 열렸어요. 쌀과 이불과 버선과 햇빛 냄새 나는 따뜻한 옷들이 쏟아졌지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The first gourd opened with a sigh. Out poured rice, blankets, socks, and warm clothes that smelled of sunlight.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 두 번째 박이 기다렸어요. “한 번 더!” 아이들이 외쳤지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The second gourd waited. “Again!” cried the children. “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

candidates.text: The second gourd waited. “Again!” cried the children. “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 두 번째 박이 기다렸어요. “한 번 더!” 아이들이 외쳤지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The second gourd waited. “Again!” cried the children. “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!”

candidates.text: 두 번째 박이 기다렸어요. “한 번 더!” 아이들이 외쳤지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라!”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서는 책과 붓과 종이와 장난감, 그리고 혼자 둥둥둥 울리는 작은 북이 나왔어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Out came books, brushes, paper, toys, and a tiny drum that played dum, dum, dum by itself.

candidates.text: Out came books, brushes, paper, toys, and a tiny drum that played dum, dum, dum by itself.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서는 책과 붓과 종이와 장난감, 그리고 혼자 둥둥둥 울리는 작은 북이 나왔어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Out came books, brushes, paper, toys, and a tiny drum that played dum, dum, dum by itself.

candidates.text: 박 속에서는 책과 붓과 종이와 장난감, 그리고 혼자 둥둥둥 울리는 작은 북이 나왔어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 세 번째 박에서는 씨앗과 연장과 약 꾸러미가 나왔어요. 잎사귀 모양 쪽지에는 이렇게 적혀 있었지요. 자라는 것은 나누어라.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The third gourd opened to seeds, tools, medicine bundles, and a note shaped like a leaf: Share what grows.

candidates.text: The third gourd opened to seeds, tools, medicine bundles, and a note shaped like a leaf: Share what grows.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 세 번째 박에서는 씨앗과 연장과 약 꾸러미가 나왔어요. 잎사귀 모양 쪽지에는 이렇게 적혀 있었지요. 자라는 것은 나누어라.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The third gourd opened to seeds, tools, medicine bundles, and a note shaped like a leaf: Share what grows.

candidates.text: 세 번째 박에서는 씨앗과 연장과 약 꾸러미가 나왔어요. 잎사귀 모양 쪽지에는 이렇게 적혀 있었지요. 자라는 것은 나누어라.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그래서 흥부는 나누었어요. 쌀은 빈 그릇으로, 이불은 추운 어깨로, 씨앗은 희망을 심을 마당으로 갔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: So Heungbu shared. Rice went to empty bowls, blankets to cold shoulders, seeds to every yard that had room for hope.

candidates.text: So Heungbu shared. Rice went to empty bowls, blankets to cold shoulders, seeds to every yard that had room for hope.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그래서 흥부는 나누었어요. 쌀은 빈 그릇으로, 이불은 추운 어깨로, 씨앗은 희망을 심을 마당으로 갔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: So Heungbu shared. Rice went to empty bowls, blankets to cold shoulders, seeds to every yard that had room for hope.

candidates.text: 그래서 흥부는 나누었어요. 쌀은 빈 그릇으로, 이불은 추운 어깨로, 씨앗은 희망을 심을 마당으로 갔지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 골목 건너편에서 놀부가 보았어요. 그는 붕대를 보지 못했고, 나눔도 보지 못했어요. 오직 박만 보았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Across the lane, Nolbu watched. He did not see the bandage. He did not see the sharing. He saw only gourds.

candidates.text: Across the lane, Nolbu watched. He did not see the bandage. He did not see the sharing. He saw only gourds.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 골목 건너편에서 놀부가 보았어요. 그는 붕대를 보지 못했고, 나눔도 보지 못했어요. 오직 박만 보았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Across the lane, Nolbu watched. He did not see the bandage. He did not see the sharing. He saw only gourds.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부는 제비를 찾았어요. 제비 한 마리가 멀쩡히 지붕에 앉자, 놀부가 너무 크게 절하는 바람에 제비는 하늘로 놀라 날아갔지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: He searched for a swallow. When one landed safely on his roof, he bowed too low and startled it into the sky.

candidates.text: He searched for a swallow. When one landed safely on his roof, he bowed too low and startled it into the sky.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부는 제비를 찾았어요. 제비 한 마리가 멀쩡히 지붕에 앉자, 놀부가 너무 크게 절하는 바람에 제비는 하늘로 놀라 날아갔지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 장바구니에서 씨앗 하나가 굴러나왔어요. “비슷하군.” 놀부가 말했어요. 그는 그 씨앗에 조급한 소원을 물처럼 주었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At last a seed rolled from a market basket. “Close enough,” said Nolbu. He planted it and watered it with impatient wishes.

candidates.text: At last a seed rolled from a market basket. “Close enough,” said Nolbu. He planted it and watered it with impatient wishes.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 장바구니에서 씨앗 하나가 굴러나왔어요. “비슷하군.” 놀부가 말했어요. 그는 그 씨앗에 조급한 소원을 물처럼 주었지요.

candidates.text: 마침내 장바구니에서 씨앗 하나가 굴러나왔어요. “비슷하군.” 놀부가 말했어요. 그는 그 씨앗에 조급한 소원을 물처럼 주었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부네 지붕에도 덩굴이 자랐어요. 그런데 잎사귀가 빗자루들이 목청을 가다듬는 소리처럼 사각거렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A vine grew on Nolbu’s roof too, but its leaves rustled like brooms clearing their throats.

candidates.text: A vine grew on Nolbu’s roof too, but its leaves rustled like brooms clearing their throats.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부네 지붕에도 덩굴이 자랐어요. 그런데 잎사귀가 빗자루들이 목청을 가다듬는 소리처럼 사각거렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A vine grew on Nolbu’s roof too, but its leaves rustled like brooms clearing their throats.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부는 너무 빨리 노래했어요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려—” 말이 끝나기도 전에 박이 펑 열렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Nolbu sang too fast: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!” The gourd popped open before he finished.

candidates.text: Nolbu sang too fast: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!” The gourd popped open before he finished.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부는 너무 빨리 노래했어요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려—” 말이 끝나기도 전에 박이 펑 열렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Nolbu sang too fast: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door!” The gourd popped open before he finished.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서 빗자루와 걸레와 비누와 양동이가 튀어나왔어요. 그것들은 놀부네 먼지 낀 마룻바닥을 영웅처럼 씻기 시작했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Out flew brooms, cloths, soap, and buckets. They scrubbed Nolbu’s dusty floors with heroic enthusiasm.

candidates.text: Out flew brooms, cloths, soap, and buckets. They scrubbed Nolbu’s dusty floors with heroic enthusiasm.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서 빗자루와 걸레와 비누와 양동이가 튀어나왔어요. 그것들은 놀부네 먼지 낀 마룻바닥을 영웅처럼 씻기 시작했지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 두 번째 박에서는 장부와 거울과 아주 엄한 주걱 하나가 나왔어요. 물건마다 놀부가 모른 척했던 사람들을 가리켰지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The second gourd released ledgers, mirrors, and one very stern rice scoop. Every item pointed toward someone Nolbu had ignored.

candidates.text: The second gourd released ledgers, mirrors, and one very stern rice scoop. Every item pointed toward someone Nolbu had ignored.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 두 번째 박에서는 장부와 거울과 아주 엄한 주걱 하나가 나왔어요. 물건마다 놀부가 모른 척했던 사람들을 가리켰지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부는 항아리 뒤에 숨으려 했어요. 항아리는 옆으로 데굴 굴렀지요. 항아리들도 닫혀 지내는 데 지쳤던 거예요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Nolbu tried to hide behind a jar. The jar rolled aside. Even his jars were tired of being closed.

candidates.text: Nolbu tried to hide behind a jar. The jar rolled aside. Even his jars were tired of being closed.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 이레 동안 놀부는 쌀을 나르고, 지붕을 고치고, 빌린 연장을 돌려주고, 아침밥 전에 덧문을 열었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: For seven days, Nolbu carried rice, patched roofs, returned tools, and opened his shutters before breakfast.

candidates.text: For seven days, Nolbu carried rice, patched roofs, returned tools, and opened his shutters before breakfast.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: For seven days, Nolbu carried rice, patched roofs, returned tools, and opened his shutters before breakfast.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 여드레째 되는 날, 흥부가 도우러 왔어요. “혼자 하지 않아도 돼.” 흥부가 말했지요. 놀부의 귀가 빨개졌어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On the eighth day, Heungbu came to help. “You do not have to do it alone,” he said. Nolbu’s ears turned red.

candidates.text: On the eighth day, Heungbu came to help. “You do not have to do it alone,” he said. Nolbu’s ears turned red.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 여드레째 되는 날, 흥부가 도우러 왔어요. “혼자 하지 않아도 돼.” 흥부가 말했지요. 놀부의 귀가 빨개졌어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On the eighth day, Heungbu came to help. “You do not have to do it alone,” he said. Nolbu’s ears turned red.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: “네 박이 갖고 싶었어.” 놀부가 인정했어요. “그런데 네 착한 마음은 내 마룻바닥을 쓸기 전까지 갖고 싶지 않았지.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: “I wanted your gourds,” Nolbu admitted. “But I did not want your kindness until it started sweeping my floor.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 흥부가 웃었어요. 놀부도 웃었지요. 오래 닫아 둔 탓인지 그의 웃음은 조금 녹슨 소리가 났어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Heungbu laughed. Nolbu laughed too, though his laugh sounded rusty from being shut away.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 둘은 마지막 박을 천천히 열었어요. 이번엔 알맞은 장단으로 노래했지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Together they opened the last gourd slowly, singing at the right pace: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 둘은 마지막 박을 천천히 열었어요. 이번엔 알맞은 장단으로 노래했지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Together they opened the last gourd slowly, singing at the right pace: “Saw, saw, little gourd — open your round door.”

candidates.text: 둘은 마지막 박을 천천히 열었어요. 이번엔 알맞은 장단으로 노래했지요. “슬근슬근 톱질하세, 둥근 박아 열려라.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서 등불과 음악과 씨앗 과자와 긴 밥상이 나왔어요. 두 집과 마을 절반이 함께 앉을 만큼 길었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Out came lanterns, music, seed cakes, and a table long enough for both houses and half the village.

candidates.text: Out came lanterns, music, seed cakes, and a table long enough for both houses and half the village.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 박 속에서 등불과 음악과 씨앗 과자와 긴 밥상이 나왔어요. 두 집과 마을 절반이 함께 앉을 만큼 길었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Out came lanterns, music, seed cakes, and a table long enough for both houses and half the village.

candidates.text: 박 속에서 등불과 음악과 씨앗 과자와 긴 밥상이 나왔어요. 두 집과 마을 절반이 함께 앉을 만큼 길었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부가 곳간을 열었어요. “들어오세요.” 그가 마을 사람들에게 말했지요. 처음엔 문이 끼익 울었고, 곧 쓸모 있게 되기를 기다렸다는 듯 후우 한숨을 쉬었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Nolbu opened his storehouse. “Come in,” he told the village. At first the door squeaked. Then it sighed, as if it had been waiting to be useful.

candidates.text: Nolbu opened his storehouse. “Come in,” he told the village. At first the door squeaked. Then it sighed, as if it had been waiting to be useful.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 놀부가 곳간을 열었어요. “들어오세요.” 그가 마을 사람들에게 말했지요. 처음엔 문이 끼익 울었고, 곧 쓸모 있게 되기를 기다렸다는 듯 후우 한숨을 쉬었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Nolbu opened his storehouse. “Come in,” he told the village. At first the door squeaked. Then it sighed, as if it had been waiting to be useful.

candidates.text: 놀부가 곳간을 열었어요. “들어오세요.” 그가 마을 사람들에게 말했지요. 처음엔 문이 끼익 울었고, 곧 쓸모 있게 되기를 기다렸다는 듯 후우 한숨을 쉬었어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그 뒤로도 흥부는 적을 때 반을 나누었고, 놀부는 곳간이 너무 가득 차기 전에 나누는 법을 배웠어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From then on, Heungbu still shared half when he had little, and Nolbu learned to share before his barns felt too full.

candidates.text: From then on, Heungbu still shared half when he had little, and Nolbu learned to share before his barns felt too full.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그 뒤로도 흥부는 적을 때 반을 나누었고, 놀부는 곳간이 너무 가득 차기 전에 나누는 법을 배웠어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From then on, Heungbu still shared half when he had little, and Nolbu learned to share before his barns felt too full.

candidates.text: 그 뒤로도 흥부는 적을 때 반을 나누었고, 놀부는 곳간이 너무 가득 차기 전에 나누는 법을 배웠어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그리고 지붕 위 박이 익을 때면 아이들은 여전히 그 노래를 불렀어요. 어떤 문은 착한 마음이 장단을 맞춰 줄 때 가장 잘 열리니까요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And when gourds ripened on the roof, children still sang the old song, because some doors open best when kindness keeps the beat.

candidates.text: And when gourds ripened on the roof, children still sang the old song, because some doors open best when kindness keeps the beat.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그리고 지붕 위 박이 익을 때면 아이들은 여전히 그 노래를 불렀어요. 어떤 문은 착한 마음이 장단을 맞춰 줄 때 가장 잘 열리니까요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And when gourds ripened on the roof, children still sang the old song, because some doors open best when kindness keeps the beat.

candidates.text: 그리고 지붕 위 박이 익을 때면 아이들은 여전히 그 노래를 불렀어요. 어떤 문은 착한 마음이 장단을 맞춰 줄 때 가장 잘 열리니까요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 눈이 마을을 하얀 이불처럼 덮었어요. 작은 부엌에서 할멈은 팥죽을 저었고, 팥죽은 작은 북처럼 보글보글 끓었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket. In a small kitchen, Grandma stirred red bean porridge until it bubbled like little drums.

candidates.text: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket. In a small kitchen, Grandma stirred red bean porridge until it bubbled like little drums.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 눈이 마을을 하얀 이불처럼 덮었어요. 작은 부엌에서 할멈은 팥죽을 저었고, 팥죽은 작은 북처럼 보글보글 끓었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket. In a small kitchen, Grandma stirred red bean porridge until it bubbled like little drums.

candidates.text: 눈이 마을을 하얀 이불처럼 덮었어요. 작은 부엌에서 할멈은 팥죽을 저었고, 팥죽은 작은 북처럼 보글보글 끓었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밖에서 우르릉 소리가 들렸어요. “할멈아! 팥죽 냄새가 난다. 나는 남의 것도 아주 잘 먹는다!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From outside came a rumble. “Grandma! I smelled porridge, and I am very good at eating things that are not mine.”

candidates.text: From outside came a rumble. “Grandma! I smelled porridge, and I am very good at eating things that are not mine.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밖에서 우르릉 소리가 들렸어요. “할멈아! 팥죽 냄새가 난다. 나는 남의 것도 아주 잘 먹는다!”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: From outside came a rumble. “Grandma! I smelled porridge, and I am very good at eating things that are not mine.”

candidates.text: 밖에서 우르릉 소리가 들렸어요. “할멈아! 팥죽 냄새가 난다. 나는 남의 것도 아주 잘 먹는다!”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 국자를 작은 달처럼 들었어요. “이 팥죽은 나누어 먹는 거지, 빼앗아 먹는 게 아니란다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma held the ladle like a tiny moon. “This porridge is for sharing,” she said, “not for snatching.”

candidates.text: Grandma held the ladle like a tiny moon. “This porridge is for sharing,” she said, “not for snatching.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 국자를 작은 달처럼 들었어요. “이 팥죽은 나누어 먹는 거지, 빼앗아 먹는 게 아니란다.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma held the ladle like a tiny moon. “This porridge is for sharing,” she said, “not for snatching.”

candidates.text: 할멈은 국자를 작은 달처럼 들었어요. “이 팥죽은 나누어 먹는 거지, 빼앗아 먹는 게 아니란다.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 문을 쿵 쳤어요. “그럼 달 뜰 때 내가 들어가겠다!” 그릇들이 달그락거리고 할멈의 숟가락이 한 번 떨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger thumped the door. “Then I will come at moonrise!” The bowls rattled. Grandma’s spoon trembled once.

candidates.text: The tiger thumped the door. “Then I will come at moonrise!” The bowls rattled. Grandma’s spoon trembled once.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 문을 쿵 쳤어요. “그럼 달 뜰 때 내가 들어가겠다!” 그릇들이 달그락거리고 할멈의 숟가락이 한 번 떨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger thumped the door. “Then I will come at moonrise!” The bowls rattled. Grandma’s spoon trembled once.

candidates.text: 호랑이가 문을 쿵 쳤어요. “그럼 달 뜰 때 내가 들어가겠다!” 그릇들이 달그락거리고 할멈의 숟가락이 한 번 떨렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 작은 달걀이 바구니에서 또르르 굴러왔어요. “용기 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 제가 문턱을 지킬게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A tiny egg rolled from the basket. “One warm spoonful for courage,” said the egg, “and I will guard the threshold.”

candidates.text: A tiny egg rolled from the basket. “One warm spoonful for courage,” said the egg, “and I will guard the threshold.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 작은 달걀이 바구니에서 또르르 굴러왔어요. “용기 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 제가 문턱을 지킬게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A tiny egg rolled from the basket. “One warm spoonful for courage,” said the egg, “and I will guard the threshold.”

candidates.text: 작은 달걀이 바구니에서 또르르 굴러왔어요. “용기 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 제가 문턱을 지킬게요.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 달걀에게 한 숟가락을 주었어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 할멈이 속삭였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma gave the egg one spoonful. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan,” she whispered.

candidates.text: Grandma gave the egg one spoonful. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan,” she whispered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 달걀에게 한 숟가락을 주었어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 할멈이 속삭였지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma gave the egg one spoonful. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan,” she whispered.

candidates.text: 할멈은 달걀에게 한 숟가락을 주었어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 할멈이 속삭였지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 멍석이 스르륵 깨어났어요. “힘 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 호랑이 발보다 빨리 빙글 돌게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The straw mat shuffled awake. “One spoonful for strength,” said the mat, “and I will spin faster than tiger paws.”

candidates.text: The straw mat shuffled awake. “One spoonful for strength,” said the mat, “and I will spin faster than tiger paws.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 멍석이 스르륵 깨어났어요. “힘 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 호랑이 발보다 빨리 빙글 돌게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The straw mat shuffled awake. “One spoonful for strength,” said the mat, “and I will spin faster than tiger paws.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 멍석에게도 팥죽을 먹였어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 부엌은 더 이상 혼자 같지 않았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma fed the mat. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.” The kitchen began to feel less lonely.

candidates.text: Grandma fed the mat. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.” The kitchen began to feel less lonely.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 멍석에게도 팥죽을 먹였어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 부엌은 더 이상 혼자 같지 않았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma fed the mat. “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.” The kitchen began to feel less lonely.

candidates.text: 할멈은 멍석에게도 팥죽을 먹였어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 부엌은 더 이상 혼자 같지 않았지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 물동이에서 자라가 고개를 빼꼼 내밀었어요. “참을성 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 기다림이 필요한 곳에서 기다릴게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A softshell turtle peeked from the water jar. “One spoonful for patience,” said Turtle, “and I will wait where waiting matters.”

candidates.text: A softshell turtle peeked from the water jar. “One spoonful for patience,” said Turtle, “and I will wait where waiting matters.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 물동이에서 자라가 고개를 빼꼼 내밀었어요. “참을성 한 숟가락만 주세요. 그러면 기다림이 필요한 곳에서 기다릴게요.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: A softshell turtle peeked from the water jar. “One spoonful for patience,” said Turtle, “and I will wait where waiting matters.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 자라에게도 한 숟가락을 주었어요. 팥죽 냄비는 조금 작아졌지만, 할멈의 용기는 더 커졌지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma gave Turtle a spoonful. The porridge pot was smaller now, but Grandma’s courage had grown larger.

candidates.text: Grandma gave Turtle a spoonful. The porridge pot was smaller now, but Grandma’s courage had grown larger.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 자라에게도 한 숟가락을 주었어요. 팥죽 냄비는 조금 작아졌지만, 할멈의 용기는 더 커졌지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 절굿공이와 국자와 작은 밤톨까지 굴러왔어요. “한 숟가락만요?” 그들이 함께 물었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pestle, the ladle, and even a small chestnut rolled close. “One spoonful?” they asked together.

candidates.text: The pestle, the ladle, and even a small chestnut rolled close. “One spoonful?” they asked together.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 절굿공이와 국자와 작은 밤톨까지 굴러왔어요. “한 숟가락만요?” 그들이 함께 물었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The pestle, the ladle, and even a small chestnut rolled close. “One spoonful?” they asked together.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 냄비를 보고, 조력자들을 보았어요. “나눈 냄비는 용감한 냄비란다.” 할멈이 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma looked at the pot. Then she looked at her helpers. “A shared pot is a brave pot,” she said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그들은 함께 계획을 세웠어요. 달걀은 문턱에, 멍석은 바닥에, 자라는 물동이 옆에, 국자는 냄비 곁에. 모두 눈처럼 조용히요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Together they made a plan: egg at the threshold, mat by the floor, Turtle by the water jar, ladle by the pot, everyone quiet as snow.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 달이 떠올랐어요. 호랑이도 왔지요. 그는 손님 하나치고는 너무 큰 웃음을 지으며 문을 밀었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Moonrise came. So did the tiger. He pushed the door open with a grin much too wide for one guest.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the threshold, the egg rolled — ddororeu! The tiger stopped so fast his whiskers wiggled.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 문턱에서 달걀이 또르르 굴렀어요. 호랑이는 너무 빨리 멈춰서 수염이 파르르 떨렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the threshold, the egg rolled — ddororeu! The tiger stopped so fast his whiskers wiggled.

candidates.text: 문턱에서 달걀이 또르르 굴렀어요. 호랑이는 너무 빨리 멈춰서 수염이 파르르 떨렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 멍석이 휙, 휙 돌았어요! 호랑이 발은 어느 쪽이 앞으로 가는 길인지 잊어버렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The mat spun — whish, whish! The tiger’s paws forgot which way was forward.

candidates.text: The mat spun — whish, whish! The tiger’s paws forgot which way was forward.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 멍석이 휙, 휙 돌았어요! 호랑이 발은 어느 쪽이 앞으로 가는 길인지 잊어버렸지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The mat spun — whish, whish! The tiger’s paws forgot which way was forward.

candidates.text: 멍석이 휙, 휙 돌았어요! 호랑이 발은 어느 쪽이 앞으로 가는 길인지 잊어버렸지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 자라는 딱 좋은 순간까지 기다렸다가 조심스러운 물 한 번을 찰박 튀겼어요. 호랑이는 찬물 그릇에 철퍼덕 앉았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Turtle waited until the perfect moment, then splashed one careful splash. The tiger sat down in a bowl of cold water.

candidates.text: Turtle waited until the perfect moment, then splashed one careful splash. The tiger sat down in a bowl of cold water.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 자라는 딱 좋은 순간까지 기다렸다가 조심스러운 물 한 번을 찰박 튀겼어요. 호랑이는 찬물 그릇에 철퍼덕 앉았지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Turtle waited until the perfect moment, then splashed one careful splash. The tiger sat down in a bowl of cold water.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 국자는 팥죽 한 방울을 떠서 호랑이 코에 얹었어요. 그건 따뜻하고 달콤해서 화내기가 어려운 맛이었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The ladle lifted one drop of porridge and placed it on the tiger’s nose. It was warm, sweet, and impossible to be angry at.

candidates.text: The ladle lifted one drop of porridge and placed it on the tiger’s nose. It was warm, sweet, and impossible to be angry at.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 국자는 팥죽 한 방울을 떠서 호랑이 코에 얹었어요. 그건 따뜻하고 달콤해서 화내기가 어려운 맛이었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The ladle lifted one drop of porridge and placed it on the tiger’s nose. It was warm, sweet, and impossible to be angry at.

candidates.text: 국자는 팥죽 한 방울을 떠서 호랑이 코에 얹었어요. 그건 따뜻하고 달콤해서 화내기가 어려운 맛이었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 눈을 깜박였어요. “냄비를 통째로 가져가려 했는데.” 그가 말했지요. “그건 참 무례했구나.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger blinked. “I was going to take the whole pot,” he said. “That was very rude of me.”

candidates.text: The tiger blinked. “I was going to take the whole pot,” he said. “That was very rude of me.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이가 눈을 깜박였어요. “냄비를 통째로 가져가려 했는데.” 그가 말했지요. “그건 참 무례했구나.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger blinked. “I was going to take the whole pot,” he said. “That was very rude of me.”

candidates.text: 호랑이가 눈을 깜박였어요. “냄비를 통째로 가져가려 했는데.” 그가 말했지요. “그건 참 무례했구나.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 팔짱을 꼈어요. “무례한 손님도 그릇을 씻으면 쓸모 있는 손님이 될 수 있지.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma folded her arms. “Rude guests may become helpful guests,” she said, “if they wash bowls.”

candidates.text: Grandma folded her arms. “Rude guests may become helpful guests,” she said, “if they wash bowls.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 할멈은 팔짱을 꼈어요. “무례한 손님도 그릇을 씻으면 쓸모 있는 손님이 될 수 있지.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Grandma folded her arms. “Rude guests may become helpful guests,” she said, “if they wash bowls.”

candidates.text: 할멈은 팔짱을 꼈어요. “무례한 손님도 그릇을 씻으면 쓸모 있는 손님이 될 수 있지.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그래서 호랑이는 그릇을 씻었어요. 하나, 다섯, 그리고 달이 졸려 보일 만큼 많이 씻었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: So the tiger washed bowls. He washed one, then five, then so many that the moon looked sleepy.

candidates.text: So the tiger washed bowls. He washed one, then five, then so many that the moon looked sleepy.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그래서 호랑이는 그릇을 씻었어요. 하나, 다섯, 그리고 달이 졸려 보일 만큼 많이 씻었지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: So the tiger washed bowls. He washed one, then five, then so many that the moon looked sleepy.

candidates.text: 그래서 호랑이는 그릇을 씻었어요. 하나, 다섯, 그리고 달이 졸려 보일 만큼 많이 씻었지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 할멈은 작은 그릇들에 팥죽을 담았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락.” 달걀이 말했어요. “슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 멍석이 말했어요. “함께 나누는 밥상.” 할멈이 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At last Grandma served porridge in small bowls. “One warm spoonful,” said the egg. “One clever plan,” said the mat. “One shared table,” said Grandma.

candidates.text: At last Grandma served porridge in small bowls. “One warm spoonful,” said the egg. “One clever plan,” said the mat. “One shared table,” said Grandma.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마침내 할멈은 작은 그릇들에 팥죽을 담았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락.” 달걀이 말했어요. “슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.” 멍석이 말했어요. “함께 나누는 밥상.” 할멈이 말했지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At last Grandma served porridge in small bowls. “One warm spoonful,” said the egg. “One clever plan,” said the mat. “One shared table,” said Grandma.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 호랑이는 가장 작은 그릇부터 들었어요. “더 먹어도 될까요?” 그가 물었지요. 모두가 놀랐다가, 모두가 웃었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: The tiger took the smallest bowl first. “May I have more?” he asked. Everyone gasped. Then everyone laughed.

candidates.text: The tiger took the smallest bowl first. “May I have more?” he asked. Everyone gasped. Then everyone laughed.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밖에는 눈이 계속 내렸어요. 안에는 팥 냄새와 깨끗한 그릇 냄새와 예절을 배우는 호랑이 냄새가 났지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Outside, snow kept falling. Inside, the kitchen smelled of red beans, clean bowls, and one tiger learning manners.

candidates.text: Outside, snow kept falling. Inside, the kitchen smelled of red beans, clean bowls, and one tiger learning manners.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밖에는 눈이 계속 내렸어요. 안에는 팥 냄새와 깨끗한 그릇 냄새와 예절을 배우는 호랑이 냄새가 났지요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그 뒤로 냄비가 보글보글 끓을 때마다 조력자들이 모였어요. 무서워서가 아니라, 나눔이 그들을 한 가족처럼 만들었기 때문이지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Whenever the pot bubbled after that, helpers gathered close. Not because they were afraid, but because sharing had made them a family.

candidates.text: Whenever the pot bubbled after that, helpers gathered close. Not because they were afraid, but because sharing had made them a family.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그 뒤로 냄비가 보글보글 끓을 때마다 조력자들이 모였어요. 무서워서가 아니라, 나눔이 그들을 한 가족처럼 만들었기 때문이지요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Whenever the pot bubbled after that, helpers gathered close. Not because they were afraid, but because sharing had made them a family.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 그리고 무례한 손님이 다시 문을 쿵쿵 두드린다면, 할멈은 무슨 말을 해야 할지 알았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.”
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And if a rude guest ever thumped at the door again, Grandma knew just what to say: “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.”

candidates.text: And if a rude guest ever thumped at the door again, Grandma knew just what to say: “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.”

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: And if a rude guest ever thumped at the door again, Grandma knew just what to say: “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.”

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 심청은 아버지의 손을 밥그릇 곁에 따뜻하게 모아 드렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the misty harbor village, first, Simcheong warmed her father's hands around a rice bowl while gulls cried over the harbor. "Lean on me," Simcheong whispered.

candidates.text: In the misty harbor village, first, Simcheong warmed her father's hands around a rice bowl while gulls cried over the harbor. "Lean on me," Simcheong whispered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 심청은 아버지의 손을 밥그릇 곁에 따뜻하게 모아 드렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the misty harbor village, first, Simcheong warmed her father's hands around a rice bowl while gulls cried over the harbor. "Lean on me," Simcheong whispered.

candidates.text: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 심청은 아버지의 손을 밥그릇 곁에 따뜻하게 모아 드렸어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 심청은 울퉁불퉁한 돌길을 세며 아버지를 이끌었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the misty harbor village, next, she counted the uneven stones so her father could cross the lane safely. "The sea is loud tonight," Father murmured.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 심청은 연꽃 등불을 놓고 바닷바람 소리를 들었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the misty harbor village, quietly, she placed a lotus lantern by his mat and listened to the sea wind. "We will find one more gentle day," Simcheong said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 안개 낀 바닷가 마을에서 마을 사람들은 떡을 가져왔지만 약단지는 거의 비어 있었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the misty harbor village, with the moon high, neighbors brought rice cakes, but Simcheong noticed her father's medicine jar was nearly empty. "I can hear your steps, my child," Father said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 장터 나루에서 뱃사람들은 바닷길을 달래는 공양 이야기를 했고 등불이 떨렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: By the market pier, first, sailors spoke of an offering to calm the sea road, and the words made the lantern tremble. "A brave heart may still shake," the harbor auntie said.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: By the market pier, quietly, she asked every auntie whether courage could be scared at the same time. "Do not call fear a failure," the auntie answered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 궁궐 계단에서 수문장도 자라도 눈을 깜빡였어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the palace steps, quietly, the guards blinked, and Turtle blinked twice as hard. "Can that be true?" Turtle whispered.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the palace steps, with the moon high, Rabbit kept his voice respectful so cleverness would not become cruelty. "You brought me in such a hurry," Rabbit answered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 궁궐 계단에서 토끼는 숲토끼가 중요한 것을 집에 둔다고 모두 아는 듯 한숨 쉬었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the palace steps, before dawn, Rabbit sighed as if everyone knew forest rabbits stored important things at home. "Then fetch it," said the court.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 다시 물가에서 토끼는 모래 위로 뛰어올라 떨리는 무릎을 쉬게 했어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back at the shore, first, Rabbit sprang onto the sand and let his knees shake where nobody could command them. "I was afraid of the king," Turtle admitted.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 다시 물가에서 자라는 물가가 둘 사이에 놓인 뒤에야 꾀를 알아차렸어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back at the shore, next, Turtle understood the ruse only after the shore was between them. "Fear still needs truth," Rabbit answered.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back at the shore, quietly, Rabbit named the wrongness without shouting. "I will say so," Turtle promised.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 다시 물가에서 물결은 자라의 사과를 궁궐 쪽으로 보냈어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back at the shore, with the moon high, the tide carried Turtle's apology back toward the palace. "A life is not a medicine jar," Rabbit said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 숲과 바다 사이에서 자라는 토끼 대신 숲의 약초를 가져갔어요.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Between forest and sea, next, the Dragon King listened because the truth had survived the tide. "No cure should steal a friend," Turtle said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 숲과 바다 사이에서 토끼는 어린 토끼들에게 등껍질에 오르기 전 이유를 묻자고 가르쳤어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Between forest and sea, quietly, Rabbit taught young hares to ask why before hopping onto any shell. "Then bring wiser help," the king replied.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 숲과 바다 사이에서 갈대에 닿는 물결은 생각하는 소리 같았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Between forest and sea, with the moon high, when waves touched the reeds, they sounded like thinking. "Think first," Rabbit told the reeds.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 소나무 산길에서 나무꾼은 추운 이웃 방을 위해 곧은 장작을 남겼어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a pine mountain path, first, the wood gatherer saved the straightest sticks for an old neighbor's cold room. "Who drums in the trees?" he wondered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 소나무 산길에서 그는 배고픈 누군가를 위해 떡 하나를 바위 위에 두었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a pine mountain path, next, he left one rice cake on a flat stone for whoever might be hungry. "Only the moon knows," he laughed.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 소나무 산길에서 빈 지게 사이로 솔바람이 피리처럼 지나갔어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a pine mountain path, quietly, pine wind moved through his empty pack like a small flute. "I brought enough to share," he called.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 달빛 숲속 공터에서 도깨비들이 삐뚤어진 모자를 쓰고 종을 울리며 튀어나왔어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the moonlit clearing, first, dokkaebi leapt from behind pine trunks with hats crooked and bells jingling. "Only if my old knees may be slow," he said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 도깨비불 곁에서 도깨비 대장이 방망이를 두드리자 따뜻한 밥그릇이 나타났어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Beside the dokkaebi fire, first, the chief dokkaebi tapped the club and a bowl of warm rice appeared. "Share twice," he answered.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 도깨비불 곁에서 나무꾼은 주머니보다 지붕을 먼저 고칠 수 있냐고 물었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Beside the dokkaebi fire, next, the wood gatherer asked whether magic could mend roofs before filling pockets. "No greedy tapping," warned the smallest.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 도깨비불 곁에서 도깨비들은 그 질문이 좋아 귀를 흔들었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Beside the dokkaebi fire, quietly, the dokkaebi liked that question so much their ears wiggled. "My hands remember," he said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마을로 돌아와 한 번 두드리자 비 새는 이웃 지붕이 고쳐졌어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back in the village, first, one tap mended the neighbor's leaking roof before rain. "The best kind can," he said.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back in the village, next, another tap filled a basket for a family with no supper. "Who fixed this?" asked the neighbor.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 마을로 돌아와 그는 칭찬이 커지지 않게 방망이를 수수한 천으로 덮었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Back in the village, quietly, he hid the club under plain cloth so praise would not grow too loud. "Maybe the moon was handy," he smiled.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the neighbor's shed, first, the neighbor snatched the club and shouted for gold before saying thank you. "Say share," whispered a dokkaebi.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 쓸어 낸 마당에서 이웃은 마당이 전보다 다정해질 때까지 쓸었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the swept courtyard, first, the neighbor swept until the courtyard looked kinder than before. "Tap once, share twice," sang the dokkaebi.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the swept courtyard, next, he carried rice to the old neighbor without asking magic to do it. "And bring rice cakes," added the smallest.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: In the swept courtyard, with the moon high, dokkaebi bells rang once, which meant they approved or wanted snacks. "Then repair twice," the wood gatherer replied.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 신성한 산에서 환웅은 구름문을 열고 사람들의 바람을 들었어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On the sacred mountain, first, Hwanung opened the cloud gate and listened to people below asking for fair weather. "Teach us," Tiger added.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On the sacred mountain, next, wind bells on the pine branches sounded like a welcome. "Patience is a path," Hwanung replied.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 동굴 입구에서 호랑이는 벌써 햇빛을 그리워하며 서성였어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the cave mouth, quietly, Tiger paced at the cave mouth, already counting the missing sunlight. "I can wait," Bear whispered.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the cave mouth, with the moon high, the stone door did not close cruelly; it simply made the promise quiet. "I can try," Tiger said.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: At the cave mouth, before dawn, Hwanung gave mugwort and garlic, plain gifts with difficult work inside. "Trying must last," said the mountain.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Inside the dim cave, first, Bear counted drips of water and made each one a small promise. "I need the sun," Tiger groaned.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Inside the dim cave, next, Tiger scratched a line in the dust, then another, then wished lines were doors. "The path is still here," Bear answered.

narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: When the waiting was complete, quietly, Hwanung called her Ungnyeo, and the cave seemed to bloom. "I was not alone," Ungnyeo answered.

candidates.text: 밝은 금강산 바위 아래에서 빗물 고인 발자국에 아이 얼굴이 비쳤어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밝은 금강산 바위 아래에서 길에서는 소나무와 돌과 찬 아침 냄새가 났어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Below bright Geumgang cliffs, with the moon high, the path smelled of pine, stone, and cold morning. "Bow first," Grandmother said.

candidates.text: Below bright Geumgang cliffs, with the moon high, the path smelled of pine, stone, and cold morning. "Bow first," Grandmother said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밝은 금강산 바위 아래에서 할머니는 솔방울을 줍기 전 산에 인사하라 가르쳤어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Below bright Geumgang cliffs, before dawn, Grandmother taught the child to greet the mountain before gathering pine cones. "To whom?" asked the child.

candidates.text: Below bright Geumgang cliffs, before dawn, Grandmother taught the child to greet the mountain before gathering pine cones. "To whom?" asked the child.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 밝은 금강산 바위 아래에서 할머니는 솔방울을 줍기 전 산에 인사하라 가르쳤어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: Below bright Geumgang cliffs, before dawn, Grandmother taught the child to greet the mountain before gathering pine cones. "To whom?" asked the child.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 좁은 소나무 길에서 호랑이는 안개처럼 조용히 바위 위로 올라왔어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a narrow pine path, first, the tiger stepped onto the rock as quietly as falling mist. "Many," said the tiger.

candidates.text: On a narrow pine path, first, the tiger stepped onto the rock as quietly as falling mist. "Many," said the tiger.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 좁은 소나무 길에서 호랑이는 안개처럼 조용히 바위 위로 올라왔어요.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 좁은 소나무 길에서 그 눈은 오래되고 밝았지만 아이를 먹잇감으로 보지 않았어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a narrow pine path, next, his eyes were old, bright, and not hungry for the child. "I am only gathering what has fallen," the child said.

candidates.text: On a narrow pine path, next, his eyes were old, bright, and not hungry for the child. "I am only gathering what has fallen," the child said.

narrationEntries.koreanNarrationText: 좁은 소나무 길에서 아이는 머리에서 솔잎이 떨어질 만큼 깊이 절했어요.
narrationEntries.englishNarrationText: On a narrow pine path, quietly, the child bowed so low a pine needle slid from their hair. "Then you know one rule," the tiger rumbled.

candidates.text: On a narrow pine path, quietly, the child bowed so low a pine needle slid from their hair. "Then you know one rule," the tiger rumbled.
